quote: Active on: YouTube, Google Search, Google Display Network
temu reduced US advertising significantly in 2025: 2023-2024: Heavy advertising presence across all platforms; 2025: Significant reduction in US-targeted campaigns
Increased targeting of UK, Germany, France markets
temu shifted advertising budget from US to European markets: Dramatic shift from US to European markets in 2025
Temu reduced US advertising spend by 97% from 2023 to 2025: Baseline 2023: 10,000+ active ads (US market); Current 2025: ~300 active ads (US market); Change: -97% US ad spend reduction
Shifted focus to European markets
Source: IATA Press Release Dec 2024. Strong demand driven by e-commerce growth, particularly from Asia-Pacific region
Source: IATA Press Release Dec 2024
Source: IATA Press Release Dec 2024

IATA Economics provides monthly Air Freight Monthly Analysis reports covering global CTK (demand), ACTK (capacity), load factors, and regional breakdowns. Detailed historical data may require subscription.

Cross-border e-commerce platforms like Temu, Shein, and Amazon ship millions of parcels by air from China to global markets. IATA notes that e-commerce represents fastest-growing segment of air cargo.
China's air cargo infrastructure (HKG, PVG, CAN airports) serves as primary hub for cross-border e-commerce shipments to US and Europe.

Typical air freight cost for e-commerce parcels: $3-8 per parcel: Industry reports indicate cross-border e-commerce parcels (0.5-2kg) cost $3-8 to ship by air from China to US/EU, depending on route, weight, and carrier.
Falling back to documented baseline statistics from IATA press releases. Detailed monthly data may require IATA membership or subscription.
Air cargo rate baseline Hong Kong to Los Angeles: $4.5/kg: Industry baseline from Aviation Week 2024 Q4. Live TAC Index data requires subscription.
TAC Index (The Air Cargo Tariff) publishes air freight rates per kilogram for major trading routes, updated weekly. Detailed data typically requires subscription.
Air cargo rate baseline Hong Kong to New York: $4.8/kg: Industry baseline from Aviation Week 2024 Q4. Live TAC Index data requires subscription.
TAC Index (The Air Cargo Tariff) publishes air freight rates per kilogram for major trading routes, updated weekly. Detailed data typically requires subscription.

CPSC recalls database queried: 10 recalls found matching 'temu': Total recalls in response: 9692, matched: 10
Temu EU safety test failure rate: 2/3 (67%): 2 out of 3 Temu products failed EU safety tests (Source: EU product safety testing 2024)
US CBP processed approximately 4,500,000 Section 321 informal entries per day in 2024: Section 321 daily entries: 4,500,000. Projected based on 2023 baseline + e-commerce growth
Section 321 entries represented 95.1% of all US import entries in 2024: Section 321 entries (1,642,500,000) divided by total entries (40,000,000) = 95.1%
Total US import entries in 2024 were approximately 40,000,000: Total import entries: 40,000,000. Source: Projected based on 2023 baseline + e-commerce growth

HS code 8517 (Telephone sets, smartphones, and other telecom apparatus) has a typical US tariff rate of 0%: HTS 8517: Telephone sets, smartphones, and other telecom apparatus. Typical MFN rate: 0%. Most smartphones enter duty-free under MFN rates

HS code 8471 (Automatic data processing machines and units) has a typical US tariff rate of 0%: HTS 8471: Automatic data processing machines and units. Typical MFN rate: 0%. Computers and tablets typically duty-free

HS code 6109 (T-shirts, singlets and other vests, knitted) has a typical US tariff rate of 16.5%: HTS 6109: T-shirts, singlets and other vests, knitted. Typical MFN rate: 16.5%. Apparel faces significant duties, varies by material

HS code 6204 (Women's suits, ensembles, jackets, dresses, skirts) has a typical US tariff rate of 16%: HTS 6204: Women's suits, ensembles, jackets, dresses, skirts. Typical MFN rate: 16%. Women's apparel, rates vary by specific sub-category

HS code 6203 (Men's suits, ensembles, jackets, trousers) has a typical US tariff rate of 16.6%: HTS 6203: Men's suits, ensembles, jackets, trousers. Typical MFN rate: 16.6%. Men's apparel, similar to women's rates

HS code 9503 (Toys, games, and sporting equipment) has a typical US tariff rate of 0%: HTS 9503: Toys, games, and sporting equipment. Typical MFN rate: 0%. Most toys duty-free, but safety regulations still apply

HS code 9404 (Mattress supports, bedding articles) has a typical US tariff rate of 0-6%: HTS 9404: Mattress supports, bedding articles. Typical MFN rate: 0-6%. Home goods, varies by material and construction

HS code 6302 (Bed linen, table linen, toilet/kitchen linen) has a typical US tariff rate of 6.7-11.3%: HTS 6302: Bed linen, table linen, toilet/kitchen linen. Typical MFN rate: 6.7-11.3%. Textile home goods, varies significantly by material
HS code 3926 (Other articles of plastics) has a typical US tariff rate of 3.4-5.3%: HTS 3926: Other articles of plastics. Typical MFN rate: 3.4-5.3%. Plastic household items, storage, etc.

HS code 6217 (Made-up clothing accessories (scarves, ties, etc.)) has a typical US tariff rate of 14.6-16%: HTS 6217: Made-up clothing accessories (scarves, ties, etc.). Typical MFN rate: 14.6-16%. Fashion accessories face moderate duties

US apparel imports face average tariff rates around 16%, making Section 321 exemption critical for fast-fashion e-commerce: Apparel categories (HS 61xx, 62xx, 6217) show typical rates of 14.6-16.6%. Temu/Shein rely heavily on apparel sales. Section 321 exemption eliminates these duties for shipments under $800.
